title: acer pectinatum subsp. forrestii
intro: Acer pectinatum subsp. forrestii, also known as the Forrest's maple, is a subspecies of the Korean maple tree. It is a deciduous tree native to China and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. The bark and leaves of the Forrest's maple contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and improving cardiovascular health. The Forrest's maple can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments.
